title: Judgment on the Prosecutor's appeal against the decision of Pre-Trial Chamber I entitled "Decision on the Prosecutor's  Application for Warrants of Arrest, Article 58"
text:
- Neither the Statute nor the Rules of Procedure and Evidence provide for what grounds can be raised on appeal pursuant to article 82 (1) (a) of the Statute. (para. 32). 

- Article 81 (1) (a) and (b) of the Statute, which provides for appeals against decisions of acquittal or conviction, specifies three categories of grounds of appeal that may be raised by the Prosecutor and four grounds of appeal that may be raised by the convicted person or the Prosecutor acting on behalf of such a person. In the absence of specification of any grounds the parties are at liberty to raise any relevant ground of appeal including the grounds as specified under article 81 (1) (a) and (b). (para. 33).

- The Prosecutor in his document in support "submits that it is appropriate to import into Article 82 the categories of error in Article 81 that can be meaningfully transposed to interlocutory appeals, namely the core errors in Article 81 (1) (a): procedural error, error of fact or error of law" […] (para. 34).

- The procedure adopted by the Prosecutor in this regard is acceptable. (para. 35).
